creator: barney@databus.com
text:
I can report some more info.  First, it's definitely not hardware,
because it happened on two different
systems and was absolutely consistent when repeated.  I have nothing
strange in make.conf.  Here;s what
I've found:

Making guile with GNU awk 3.0.6 as /usr/bin/awk works fine.
Making guile with 3.1.0 as /usr/local/bin/gawk works fine.
Makeing guile with 3.0.6 as /usr/local/bin/gawk coredumps.

BUT - the /usr/local/bin/gawk 3.0.6, built from /usr/ports/lang/gawk is
163120 bytes, while the same
version in /usr/bin/awk is 143244.  I have no explanation for this
difference, and cannot easily
recreate the build environment from 6/26/01 when I built gawk-3.0.6
because I've cvsupped and
built world a couple of times since then.

Since whatever was wrong appears to be gone, I'd support closing this
PR.  I can send the bad gawk
and coredump if anybody is interested, but if it were up to me I'd just
drop it.

Thanks,
Barney
